content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms like ICM and RND can be re-framed as a self-supervised 'episodic memory pruning' signal for long-lived autonomous agents, where only unexpected, high-curiosity interactions are preserved in the agent's working memory to avoid catastrophic clutter. This creates a fully autonomous self-improving loop where the agent's own innate curiosity drives both what it learns and what it remembers, without requiring external labeled data.
